 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-88b","title":"security.sh: avoid printing hash on fetch failure","description":"scripts/lib/security.sh fetch_checksum currently can emit the SHA of empty input when curl fails (because sha256sum still runs). Change it to only emit a hash when fetch succeeds (preserve exact bytes with sentinel).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T13:35:36.17546-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:36:44.037785-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:36:44.037785-05:00","close_reason":"Updated scripts/lib/security.sh fetch_checksum to only emit a checksum when curl fetch succeeds (preserves trailing newlines via sentinel), avoiding accidental empty-input hashes on network failure."}

-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8mv","title":"Add --strict flag for legacy checksum behavior","description":"# Task: Add --strict flag for legacy checksum behavior

## Context
Part of EPIC: Checkpoint-Based Checksum Recovery (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tx7)

## What to Do
Add --strict flag that restores the original behavior where ANY checksum mismatch aborts installation.

### Behavior
- Without --strict (default): Use new recovery flow
- With --strict: All tools treated as critical, any mismatch aborts

### Use Cases
- Security-conscious users who want no exceptions
- CI/CD environments where reproducibility matters
- Auditing/compliance scenarios

## Acceptance Criteria
- --strict flag parsed in argument handling
- When set, all tools treated as critical
- Help text documents the flag
- Default behavior is recovery flow

## Files to Modify
- install.sh: Argument parsing, pass flag to security functions","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:44:39.830135-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:03:22.362403-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8mv","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-v8a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:33.579339-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8xx","title":"End-to-end test: acfs-update","description":"## What
Comprehensive testing of acfs-update:
1. Test on fresh ACFS install (all tools present)
2. Test on partial install (some tools missing)
3. Test each category individually
4. Test --dry-run mode
5. Test --yes mode (non-interactive)
6. Test failure handling (simulate failures)
7. Test logging output

## Test Scenarios
- Fresh VPS with full ACFS install
- Existing VPS with previous ACFS version
- Missing tools (should skip gracefully)
- Network failures (should report and continue)
- Permission issues (should report and continue)

## Considerations
- Need a test VPS or Docker container
- Some tests may need mocking (network failures)
- Should test as both root and non-root user

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All categories update correctly
- [ ] Dry-run shows accurate preview
- [ ] Failures handled gracefully
- [ ] Logs are comprehensive and useful
- [ ] No regressions in existing functionality","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:10.04603-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:30:57.16676-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8xx","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-csv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:35.23095-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8z5","title":"Create report_skipped_tools() post-install summary","description":"# Task: Create report_skipped_tools() post-install summary

## Context
Part of EPIC: Checkpoint-Based Checksum Recovery (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tx7)

## What to Do
After installation completes, report any tools that were skipped:

### Output
```
⚠ The following tools were skipped due to checksum mismatches:
    → ntm: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh
    → bv: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh

You can install these manually after verifying they are safe.
Or wait for ACFS to update checksums and run: acfs update --stack
```

### Storage
- SKIPPED_TOOLS array populated during install
- Also saved to state.json for persistence

## Acceptance Criteria
- Only shown if SKIPPED_TOOLS is non-empty
- Shows tool name and installer URL
- Provides manual install command
- Mentions acfs update as alternative

## Files to Modify
- install.sh: Add report at end of successful install","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:44:38.550459-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:48:42.459331-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:48:42.459331-05:00","close_reason":"Added comprehensive skipped tools reporting: record_skipped_tool() with reason and URL, report_skipped_tools() showing detailed summary with manual install commands and acfs update alternative, get_skipped_tools_json() for state persistence.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8z5","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4jr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:47:33.367598-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-pom","title":"Implement ACFS self-update","description":"## What
Before updating other components, acfs-update should update itself:
1. git pull in ~/.acfs (or wherever ACFS is installed)
2. Re-source the update script if it changed
3. Continue with other updates using latest logic

## Why This Matters
- Ensures we have the latest update logic before updating tools
- Fixes any bugs in the update process itself
- Keeps checksums.yaml current (though not used for updates)

## Technical Details
- ACFS installed at ~/.acfs or detected via ACFS_HOME env var
- git pull --ff-only to avoid merge conflicts
- If update script changed, need to re-exec with same args

## Considerations
- What if git pull fails? (network, conflicts)
- Should we require this, or make it optional?
- Re-exec is tricky - need to preserve all args and state

## Decision
Make self-update automatic but non-fatal. If it fails, warn and continue with current version.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] ACFS self-updates on each run
- [ ] Handles git pull failures gracefully
- [ ] Re-exec works if script changed (or skip re-exec v1)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:45.271437-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:31:43.349328-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T13:31:43.349328-05:00","close_reason":"Out of scope for v1. ACFS is not git-cloned to ~/.acfs, so self-update requires re-running the installer. Users can do this manually. Consider for v2 after changing install to use git clone.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-pom","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.670496-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-q10","title":"Harden installer: true dry-run, onboard source, HTTPS PGDG","description":"Fix install.sh --dry-run so it makes no system changes and works without sudo; install onboard command from packages/onboard/onboard.sh (single source of truth); switch PGDG apt repo URL to HTTPS; fix checksum computation in scripts/lib/security.sh.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"FuchsiaCreek","created_at":"2025-12-20T13:18:40.054877-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T13:22:16.261425-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T13:22:16.261425-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed install.sh dry-run to make no system changes + allow running without sudo; install onboard from packages/onboard; switch PGDG repo to HTTPS; fix security.sh checksum logic."}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qqo","title":"Create error context tracking globals and try_step() wrapper","description":"# Task: Create error context tracking globals and try_step() wrapper

## Context
Part of EPIC: Per-Phase Error Reporting (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-fkf)

## What to Do
Add error context tracking to capture exactly where failures occur:

### Global Variables
```bash
CURRENT_PHASE=""
CURRENT_PHASE_NAME=""
CURRENT_STEP=""
LAST_ERROR=""
LAST_ERROR_CODE=""
```

### try_step() Function
Wrapper for individual operations within a phase:
```bash
try_step "description" command args...
# - Sets CURRENT_STEP
# - Saves state (for resume granularity)
# - Captures output and exit code
# - On failure: sets LAST_ERROR, returns 1
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every installation command wrapped with try_step
- Error output captured (not just exit code)
- Current step visible in state.json for debugging
- Works with pipes and complex commands

## Implementation Notes
- Use eval for complex commands
- Capture both stdout and stderr
- Truncate very long errors for display

## Files to Modify
- install.sh: Add globals and try_step function","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:43:57.840225-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:43:08.613653-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:43:08.613653-05:00","close_reason":"Created scripts/lib/error_tracking.sh with try_step(), try_step_optional(), try_step_retry(), phase management, and error reporting functions"}
-{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qup","title":"Show skipped tools in acfs doctor output","description":"# Task: Show skipped tools in acfs doctor output

## Purpose
When doctor runs, clearly show which tools were intentionally skipped vs which failed unexpectedly.

## Critical Distinction: Intentional Skip vs Bug

**Problem:** Without this distinction, doctor output is confusing:
- User intentionally skipped `ohmyzsh` via checksum prompt
- Doctor shows: `[✗] ohmyzsh: not found`
- User thinks: "Did installation break?"

**Solution:** Track skip reasons and display appropriately.

### Skip Tracking in state.json
```json
{
  "skipped_tools": {
    "ohmyzsh": {
      "reason": "checksum_mismatch",
      "skipped_at": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z",
      "user_choice": "skip"
    },
    "atuin": {
      "reason": "network_error",
      "skipped_at": "2025-01-15T10:32:00Z",
      "retries": 3
    }
  }
}
```

### Doctor Output Format
```
ACFS Doctor Report
==================

Installed Tools:
  [✓] mise 2024.12.0
  [✓] uv 0.5.0
  [✓] bun 1.2.0
  [✓] go 1.23.0
  ...

Intentionally Skipped:
  [○] ohmyzsh - skipped (checksum mismatch, user choice)
  [○] atuin - skipped (network error after 3 retries)

Missing (unexpected):
  [✗] lazygit - not found (should be installed by mise)
  [✗] starship - not found

Legend: [✓] installed  [○] skipped  [✗] missing
```

### Key Design Decisions

1. **Use `[○]` for skipped** - visually distinct from `[✓]` (success) and `[✗]` (error)
2. **Show skip reason** - user knows WHY it was skipped
3. **Separate section** - dont mix intentional skips with failures
4. **Missing = unexpected** - tools that SHOULD exist but dont

### Implementation
```bash
show_tool_status() {
    local tool="$1"
    local state_file="$ACFS_STATE_DIR/state.json"
    
    # Check if intentionally skipped
    if jq -e ".skipped_tools.\"$tool\"" "$state_file" &>/dev/null; then
        local reason=$(jq -r ".skipped_tools.\"$tool\".reason" "$state_file")
        echo "[○] $tool - skipped ($reason)"
        return
    fi
    
    # Check if installed
    if command -v "$tool" &>/dev/null; then
        local version=$("$tool" --version 2>/dev/null | head -1 || echo "installed")
        echo "[✓] $tool $version"
    else
        echo "[✗] $tool - not found"
    fi
}
```

## Dependencies
- Depends on: state.json schema (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5zt)
- Used by: doctor output formatting (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-aqs)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Skipped tools tracked in state.json with reason
- [ ] Doctor uses [○] symbol for intentional skips
- [ ] Separate "Intentionally Skipped" section in output
- [ ] Skip reason displayed (checksum/network/user choice)
- [ ] Clear legend explaining symbols","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:50:40.525291-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:00:14.222777-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-qup","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-8z5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:29.321854-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ss3","title":"Add --json output mode to security.sh --verify","description":"# Task: Add --json output mode to security.sh --verify

## Context
Part of EPIC: Automated Upstream Checksum Monitoring (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-4ou)

## What to Do
Enhance security.sh --verify to output JSON for automation:

### Command
```bash
./scripts/lib/security.sh --verify --json
```

### Output Format
```json
{
    "timestamp": "2025-01-15T06:00:00Z",
    "total": 14,
    "matches": [
        {"name": "bun", "checksum": "abc123..."}
    ],
    "mismatches": [
        {
            "name": "ntm",
            "expected": "abc123...",
            "actual": "def456...",
            "url": "https://..."
        }
    ],
    "errors": [
        {"name": "broken", "error": "Fetch failed"}
    ]
}
```

### Implementation
Create verify_all_json() function that builds JSON output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- --json flag produces valid JSON
- All checksums verified and categorized
- Mismatches include old and new values
- Errors captured (network failures, etc.)

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/security.sh","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:45:51.791796-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:48:46.932748-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:48:46.932748-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented verify_all_installers_json() function with --json flag for automated verification.  {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t9i","title":"EPIC: Enhanced Doctor with Functional Tests","description":"# Epic: Enhanced Doctor with Functional Tests

## Overview
Add --deep flag to acfs doctor that performs functional smoke tests, not just binary existence checks.

## Problem Statement
acfs doctor only checks if binaries exist, not if they work. It says 'All critical checks passed' but cc immediately fails with 'not authenticated'. Binary existence ≠ functional.

## Business Impact
- Auth issues are the most common post-install failure
- Users copy aliases but skip authentication
- Deep checks catch drift (tokens expire, configs corrupted)
- Reduces 'it says it passed but nothing works' support tickets

## Technical Approach
1. Add --deep flag (default doctor stays fast)
2. Agent auth checks: Claude config exists + test call, Codex OPENAI_API_KEY, Gemini credentials
3. Database checks: PostgreSQL connection and role verification
4. Cloud CLI checks: Vault configured, Wrangler/Supabase/Vercel auth status
5. Token expiration warnings where applicable
6. Cache results to avoid rate limiting

## Success Criteria
- --deep catches all auth configuration issues
- Each check has actionable fix suggestion
- 4 status levels: pass, warn, fail, info (skip)
- Maintains backwards compatibility (default doctor unchanged)

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/doctor.sh: Add --deep flag and functional tests

## Reference
See: docs/ROADMAP_INSTALLER_RELIABILITY.md Section 5","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:41:13.234724-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T12:41:13.234724-05:00","comments":[{"id":5,"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-t9i","author":"jemanuel","text":"FYI: created `agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt` (Manifest-Driven Installer Integration). This work will refactor `install.sh` to source generated installers + manifest_index and will touch selection semantics (`--only/--skip/--print-plan`) and curl|bash bootstrap (single archive).

Please keep this epic’s work compatible with your scope:
- orchestration/state/error-reporting/preflight remains install.sh + scripts/lib owned
- generated scripts stay source-safe (no top-level side effects)","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:50:07Z"}]}
